title: Cross-Cloud Service Selection Workbook
subtitle: A solution-engineering decision system for AWS, Microsoft Azure and Google Cloud.

steps.title: Frame the outcome
steps.action: Write the business outcome, users, data, scale, security, SLA and budget constraints.
steps.guardrail: Do not begin with a product name.

steps.title: Locate the layer
steps.action: Use Architecture Map to identify the layer and subcategory.
steps.guardrail: This reduces hundreds of services into a small decision set.

steps.title: Start from a default
steps.action: Use Quick Picks for common scenarios, or filter Service Decision Matrix.
steps.guardrail: Defaults reduce analysis paralysis but remain challengeable.

steps.title: Compare trade-offs
steps.action: Use Decision Assistant and adjust criteria weights and ecosystem context.
steps.guardrail: A small score difference means context matters more than the rating.

steps.title: Validate and record
steps.action: Check region, SLA, security, price, quotas and proof of value; record an ADR.
steps.guardrail: A recommendation is not an approval.

guides.what: Browse 24 architecture domains and 307 capability families.
guides.when: Begin here when defining scope or checking whether a category is missing.

guides.what: Decompose Unity Catalog, Microsoft Foundry, SageMaker, Bedrock and Gemini Enterprise into 55 internal capabilities.
guides.when: Use when designing AI platforms, agents, RAG, MLOps or AI governance.

guides.what: Apply 33 lifecycle controls across ownership, data, identity, safety, evaluation, monitoring and incident response.
guides.when: Use for governance workshops, control design and architecture assurance.

guides.what: Use 28 reusable evidence artifacts for audits, AI Act/GDPR readiness, NIST AI RMF and ISO/IEC 42001 alignment.
guides.when: Use to build the AI assurance evidence pack.

guides.what: Compare 31 SQL, NoSQL, vector, graph, cache, ledger and analytical data-store decisions.
guides.when: Use before selecting RDS, Cosmos DB, Spanner, DynamoDB, Firestore and related services.

guides.what: Filter the 268 scored capabilities using the expanded taxonomy.
guides.when: Use for detailed AWS, Azure and Google Cloud service selection.

guides.what: See which domains are strong, partial, limited or missing.
guides.when: Use to prevent false confidence in incomplete areas.

guides.what: Review unscored capabilities that still need detailed provider decisions.
guides.when: Use before calling a domain decision-complete.

guides.what: Apply reliability, security, cost, performance, data and responsible-AI lenses.
guides.when: Use during architecture reviews and client decision workshops.
